divide.detail: Broadcast Div [3072,256]/[3072,1] fails at backend CompileFunction in libtile_fwk_interface.so. Minimal 2D Div [1,32]/[1,1] passes. API pypto.op.div() exists and works for non-broadcast cases. The failure is specifically at the backend compilation pass when handling broadcast pattern.
divide.workaround_possible: Use element-wise Div without broadcast, perform broadcast manually

equal.detail: pypto.eq in pypto.op.comparison fails to correctly lower comparison results. Even minimal [1,32] FP16->FP16 produces wrong results (sum=0.0002 vs expected 32.0). Full shape fails at CompileFunction Expand pass. The backend produces raw packed bitmask reinterpreted as FP16 instead of proper 0/1 output.
equal.workaround_possible: Not fixable from user code; requires PyPTO backend fix

where.detail: pypto.where (condition, x1, x2) fails at CompileFunction with 'size of tensor a (384) must match size of tensor b (3072)'. Root cause: PyPTO tiling Expand pass misinterprets condition physical size when condition dtype (uint8) differs from data dtype (float16). Same-shape condition still fails.
where.workaround_possible: Not fixable from user code; requires PyPTO backend fix for mixed-dtype where

ascendc_expand.detail: The Ascend C kernel reads [B,256,1] input, expands to [B,256,384] in CPU memory, then copies to device. The actual expand logic is done on CPU, not in the kernel. The kernel is essentially a data-movement copy kernel.
ascendc_expand.workaround_possible: Rewrite kernel to perform broadcast on-device using Duplicate API

ascendc_transpose.detail: The host code performs the actual transpose on CPU then copies to device. The kernel copies already-transposed data as identity. The transpose is not done on device.
ascendc_transpose.workaround_possible: Rewrite kernel to perform transpose on-device using 16x16 tile-based approach

ascendc_reduce_sum.detail: The host code performs FP32 pre-reduction on CPU and copies pre-reduced data to the kernel input buffer. The kernel then reduces already-reduced data. Actual compute is duplicated on CPU and device.
ascendc_reduce_sum.workaround_possible: Remove CPU pre-reduction; let kernel handle full reduce from raw FP16 data

ascendc_where_scalar.detail: The kernel uses per-element scalar loop for ternary select because vectorized arithmetic approach (cond*x1+(1-cond)*x2) failed due to Cast<uint8,half> not producing exact 0.0/1.0 and NaN propagation. The scalar loop is 37x slower than vectorized Not/Or kernels.
ascendc_where_scalar.workaround_possible: Investigate vectorized Select API availability on dav-2201

pypto_or_bitwise.detail: PyPTO Or uses pypto.bitwise_or(x1, x2) instead of pypto.op.logical_or(x1, x2). For strict 0/1 inputs, results are identical. For non-0/1 uint8 values, bitwise_or gives different results (e.g., uint8 2|3=3 instead of logical 1). Spec requires logical OR semantics. Current test data happens to be strictly 0/1.
pypto_or_bitwise.workaround_possible: Change to pypto.op.logical_or if available, or ensure inputs are 0/1 normalized first

pypto_not_flat_latency.detail: PyPTO Not latency is flat (~130-137 us) across all batch sizes B=1..64. This is inconsistent with a device kernel processing varying data volumes. Likely measuring host dispatch overhead, not actual kernel time. No msprof data to verify.
pypto_not_flat_latency.workaround_possible: Collect msprof data to verify kernel timing vs host dispatch

pypto_or_flat_latency.detail: PyPTO Or latency is flat (~142-149 us) across all batch sizes B=1..64. Same suspicious pattern as Not. No msprof data. Measurement uses torch.npu.Event timing, not msprof.
pypto_or_flat_latency.workaround_possible: Collect msprof data to verify kernel timing vs host dispatch
